quince-jam-with-vanilla-argiro.name: Quince Jam With Vanilla
instructions: Rub them in a thick scrubber or cut them into small pieces.
Place them in a pot and pour with the water.
We collect peels and hearts, tie them to a tulpani and boil them with the fruits.
Allow them to boil and then boil for about 15 minutes.
Remove the tulip with the peels (throw after draining well).
If we cut the quinces into pieces, we mash them with a stick-blader.
Then add the sugar, lemon zest, torn vertical vanilla and mix regularly with a wooden spoon, and let it boil for about 45 minutes in medium heat.
As the jam boils and the sugar caramelizes, it will also brown its color, and in the end it will have a soft red color.
The quince jam is ready when it leaves a thick layer on the back of the spoon.
We split into two sterile jars.

quinoa-salad-with-peaches-and-pickled-on.name: Quinoa Salad With Peaches And Pickled Onions

ingredients.prep: thick stems trimmed, leaves torn (about 2 cups)

instructions: Bring quinoa and 4 cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Season with salt. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer until quinoa is tender, 8–10 minutes. Drain, return quinoa to pan, and cover. Remove from heat and let sit 15 minutes. Fluff with a fork and spread out on a rimmed baking sheet; let cool.

Meanwhile, place onion in a small bowl. Bring vinegar, sugar, and 4 tsp. salt to a boil in a small saucepan, stirring to dissolve sugar and salt. Pour over onion and let stand 20 minutes. Drain, reserving pickling liquid.

Toss pickled onion, peaches, arugula, tomatoes, oil, ¼ cup chives, and 3 Tbsp. reserved pickling liquid in a large bowl; season with salt, pepper, and more pickling liquid, if desired. Fold in quinoa.
Serve salad topped with remaining ¼ cup chives.
DO AHEAD: Quinoa salad can be made 6 hours ahead (do not add arugula and chives). Cover and chill. Bring to room temperature and toss in arugula and chives just before serving.
